title: Fans Predict Third Trailer Release on August 7, 2026
summary: Players believe the upcoming Take-Two earnings call on August 7 will include the reveal of a third GTA 6 trailer. Speculation suggests both a cinematic trailer and gameplay footage could arrive before the earnings report, following the pattern from previous Rockstar releases. Many fans have grown frustrated by the 15-month wait since the second trailer.

title: Sony's Physical Disc Ban Generates More Views Than GTA 6 Trailers
summary: Sony's announcement to end physical disc production by January 2028 has gained nearly 140 million views, surpassing the first GTA 6 trailer. The controversy has overshadowed GTA 6 marketing, with the backlash receiving over 100,000 critical replies on social media. This comes as GTA 6 itself ships without a disc, using download codes instead.

title: Sony Kept Publishers and Internal Teams in the Dark About Disc Decision
summary: Reports reveal Sony did not warn major publishers or even its own regional offices before announcing the end of physical discs. A publisher executive stated they learned about the policy the same time as the public. Sony's India office was particularly affected, having actively negotiated new retail store deals without knowing discs would be discontinued.

title: Rockstar Developers Threaten Strike Action Before November Launch
summary: Developers at Rockstar's UK studios are moving forward with unionization efforts amid the final crunch period before GTA 6's November 19 release. Workers cite concerns over mandatory overtime, pay transparency, and working conditions. A potential strike during the critical development phase could delay the game or result in technical issues at launch.

title: PlayStation Removes GTA 6 Marketing Amid Physical Media Backlash
summary: Sony scaled back promotional materials for GTA 6 on its platforms after facing intense criticism over ending physical disc support. The PlayStation app removed its tropical Vice City-themed branding and PS5 dashboards no longer display Leonida banners. While the removal may have been scheduled, the timing fueled speculation that Sony wanted to distance itself from the controversy.
